mission.title: flux: GitLab bootstrap - git clone error: repository not found
mission.description: GitLab bootstrap - git clone error: repository not found. This issue affects 6+ users.

steps.title: Identify flux troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your flux deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=flux
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `failed to find group named 'company-de'`

steps.title: Check current Deployment configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux resources:
```bash
kubectl get deployment -A
kubectl describe deployment <name> -n <namespace>
```
### Describe the bug

I am using a paid gitlab.com. The site reports this version: GitLab Enterprise Edition 13.9.0-pre 04013f0902b.
I am using flux2 v0.8.0.

I have a private group called 'company-de

steps.title: Apply the fix for GitLab bootstrap - git clone error: repository not found
steps.description:

providers like GitHub from `fluxcd/pkg/git` to `fluxcd/go-git-providers`,
eliminating quite some bugs that were present in the GitLab integration.

In addition to this, the bootstrap logic has been factored out into an

steps.title: Confirm GitLab bootstrap - git clone error: repository not is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `failed to find group named 'company-de'` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: This pull request changes the implementation used to connect to Git
providers like GitHub from `fluxcd/pkg/git` to `fluxcd/go-git-providers`,
eliminating quite some bugs that were present in the GitLab integration.

In addition to this, the bootstrap logic has been factored out into an
(for now) internal package with two bootstrap "runners" `PlainGitBootstrapper`
and `GitProviderBootstrapper`. The. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

I changed the repo name so it doesnt contain a dash. I tried to bootstrap one level up in 'project' (so its not a subgroup in a subgroup). It didnt help. I dont see what is the problem.
